mainEntity.name: 1.  Why did you want to gain a distinction with the Royal Photographic Society (RPS)?

acceptedAnswer.text: I was actually talked into it by a friend who wanted support to gain the distinction. Many years ago, I had completed an A-level, resulting in an A*, which is equivalent to the LRPS distinction, so I thought I would try as well. By previously doing the A-level, I had some idea of what would be required, although without the support of a tutor.

mainEntity.name: 2.  Why did you choose Alan to mentor you for a distinction and had you worked with Alan before the RPS Distinctions Mentoring Course?

acceptedAnswer.text: I was already on Alan’s monthly mentoring scheme, so it was a natural progression to work towards the distinction with him.

mainEntity.name: 4. Do you feel you got the right level and input of support from the course?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, Alan was supportive in advising me about my images, arranging and rearranging the panel and printing

mainEntity.name: 6.  What distinction did you apply for and were you successful?

acceptedAnswer.text: I applied for and gained the LRPS

mainEntity.name: 7.  What was your experience like of the RPS Assessment Day?

acceptedAnswer.text: I attended on Zoom, I was quite nervous but also confident that Alan had guided me to do my best. It was one of the last print assessments and I felt the print submissions were of a higher quality compared with many of the digital submissions.

mainEntity.name: 9. Having completed the course and Distinction Assessment, what advice would you like to share with others?

acceptedAnswer.text: Definitely get a mentor for guidance, and attend an Advisory Day or a One-to-One with the RPS.

mainEntity.name: 11.  Has working for a distinction helped you progress as a photographer?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes I feel my photography has improved immensely and I have more confidence.

mainEntity.name: 12. Any other comments you would like to add?

acceptedAnswer.text:
I am not sure if I would have achieved the distinction without Alan's support and guidance. At times, I felt confident, and at other times, I did not. I felt I could rely on Alan’s knowledge to get me through the process. I was grateful that Alan took the time to see my prints printed before they were sent to me.
